panels.description: Wide shot of a 19th-century drawing room. Mr. Bennet sits in an armchair with a smirk, watching Mr. Collins, who is standing and speaking pompously with dramatic gestures. Elizabeth sits nearby, amused. Warm, soft indoor lighting.

bubbles.text: Mr. Bennet listened to his absurd cousin with keen enjoyment, keeping a resolute composure.

panels.description: Medium shot. Mr. Collins recoils in horror, holding out a book like it's poisonous. Lydia and Kitty look on with wide-eyed expressions of shock.

bubbles.text: I protest! I never read novels!

panels.description: Close up on Lydia, looking bored and disruptive, leaning forward with her mouth open. Mr. Collins is in the background, out of focus, reading from a thick sermon book.

bubbles.text: Do you know, mamma, that my uncle Philips talks of turning away Richard?

panels.description: Medium close up. Mr. Collins looks deeply offended, holding his book shut. He stares down his nose with a haughty expression. Elizabeth looks on with an annoyed, weary face.

bubbles.text: It amazes me. There is nothing so advantageous as instruction.

panels.description: Wide shot. Mr. Collins and Mr. Bennet sit at a small table playing backgammon, looking satisfied. The ladies in the background apologize politely, appearing relieved that the ordeal is over.

bubbles.text: Wise to leave the girls to their trifles, eh cousin?
